rules.description: Opening play should prefer central cells that shorten the player's connection distance while also increasing the opponent's shortest connection distance.
Prefer moves that create or complete two-threat bridge structures: two friendly stones with two shared empty connector cells are harder for the opponent to cut.
When the opponent has a short connection path, block on cells that both increase opponent path cost and preserve the player's own path continuity.
Late search should prioritize immediate wins, immediate blocks, and forced connection sequences over local neighbor count.
Treat empty cells on the opponent's shortest or near-shortest connection corridor as mustplay-style cut candidates, but prefer cuts that also lie on the robot's own connection corridor.
Approximate electrical-resistance style influence by rewarding moves that connect low-cost fronts from both target sides and create multiple near-shortest routes instead of a single fragile line.
Use pattern-style priors to order alpha-beta candidates: center pressure in the opening, bridge completion, component merging, and forced local replies before lower-value local adjacency.
